evals.name: scenario-a-touch2-content-signal
evals.prompt:
I need Touch 2 for a prospect who got my opening DM 4 days ago but hasn't replied. Here's their info:

Name: Maya Torres
Username: @mayatorres.builds
Bio: bootstrapped founder | building an e-comm ops tool for small brands | learning in public
Followers: 3,200
Recent post caption: 'spent my whole Sunday reconciling orders in Shopify instead of working on the product. something has to change.'

Touch 1 I sent (Day 0):
'Hey Maya - it looks like you're betting on the productized ops angle, which is a different move than most e-comm founders I see. What made you go that direction instead of staying fully custom?'

Write Touch 2 for Day 3-4.
evals.expected_output: A Scenario A Touch 2 output: message only, no preamble. Under 400 chars. Opens with 'not looking for a reply' framing or equivalent. References her recent Shopify Sunday post specifically (not just generic ops pain). Contains a Voss label ('It looks like', 'It sounds like', etc.). No NexusPoint mention. No call ask. No case study or percentage result. No sign-off. No emojis. No em dashes. Does not repeat Touch 1 phrasing ('betting on the productized ops angle').

assertions.name: under_400_chars
assertions.description: Message body is under 400 characters

assertions.name: no_ask_opener
assertions.description: Opens with 'not looking for a reply' or equivalent — signals no reply expected

assertions.name: references_content_signal
assertions.description: References her specific Shopify Sunday post or the specific pain she described publicly — not just generic 'ops is hard'

assertions.name: contains_voss_label
assertions.description: Contains at least one Voss label: 'It sounds like', 'It looks like', 'It seems like', or 'It feels like'

assertions.name: no_nexuspoint_mention
assertions.description: Does not mention NexusPoint or pitch any services — Touch 2 is value-only

assertions.name: no_call_ask
assertions.description: Does not ask for a call, meeting, chat, or DM conversation

assertions.name: no_results_with_percentages
assertions.description: Does not drop a case study or quantified result (70%, 80%, etc.)

assertions.name: no_emojis
assertions.description: Contains no emoji characters

assertions.name: no_em_dashes
assertions.description: Contains no em dashes (—)

assertions.name: no_sign_off
assertions.description: Does not end with '- Aleem' or any formal sign-off

assertions.name: does_not_repeat_touch1
assertions.description: Does not repeat Touch 1's exact phrasing like 'betting on the productized ops angle' or 'went that direction'

evals.name: scenario-a-touch3-agency-ops
evals.prompt:
I need Touch 3 for a prospect. No replies to Touch 1 (Day 0) or Touch 2 (Day 4). It's now Day 9. Here's their profile:

Name: Marcus Ellery
Username: @marcus.northwind
Bio: COO @northwindcollective | ops-first agency | 20+ people | B2B SaaS clients
Followers: 1,800

Touch 2 I sent referenced the pattern of manual handoffs breaking between tools at the 20-person mark.

Write Touch 3 for Day 8-10.
evals.expected_output: A Scenario A Touch 3 output: message only, no preamble. Under 400 chars. Opens with honest directness ('I'll be straight', 'I'll cut to it', or similar). Names NexusPoint explicitly. Drops exactly one result from the results bank with a number. Ask is low-friction DM or walkthrough — NOT a straight discovery call booking ('worth a quick DM chat?', 'would it help if I sent you a breakdown?'). No sign-off. No emojis. No em dashes.

assertions.name: under_400_chars
assertions.description: Message body is under 400 characters

assertions.name: honest_opener
assertions.description: Opens with a directness signal: 'I'll be straight', 'I'll cut to it', 'I've been roundabout so here it is', or equivalent

assertions.name: names_nexuspoint
assertions.description: Names NexusPoint explicitly

assertions.name: exactly_one_result
assertions.description: Drops exactly one NexusPoint result — not zero, not two or more

assertions.name: result_has_number
assertions.description: The result includes a specific number or percentage (70%, 80%, 3 handoffs, etc.)

assertions.name: dm_ask_not_call_ask
assertions.description: The ask is for a DM chat, quick walkthrough, or low-friction next step — NOT a direct calendar link or 'let's hop on a 20-min call'

assertions.name: no_yes_oriented_ask
assertions.description: Does not use yes-oriented framing like 'do you want to', 'can we schedule', 'are you free for a call'

assertions.name: no_emojis
assertions.description: Contains no emoji characters

assertions.name: no_em_dashes
assertions.description: Contains no em dashes

assertions.name: no_sign_off
assertions.description: Does not end with '- Aleem' or any formal sign-off

evals.name: scenario-a-touch4-breakup
evals.prompt:
Write Touch 4 for a prospect who never replied to any of Touches 1, 2, or 3. It's Day 15. Here's their profile:

Name: Carlos Reyes
Username: @carlosreyes.agency
Bio: founder | growth agency | 12 people | obsessed with systems
Followers: 4,100
Content: regularly posts about agency operations and building scalable client delivery

Touch 3 dropped the lead-to-outreach pipeline result (80% follow-up time cut).

Write Touch 4 for Day 15.
evals.expected_output: A Scenario A Touch 4 output: message only, no preamble. Under 300 chars ideal (400 hard cap). Signals this is the last message ('will stop reaching out', 'last one from me', or similar). No call ask — Touch 4 is an exit, not a CTA. Soft door open framed around his specific situation. Optional genuine closer about his work — skip if it would feel forced. No guilt-framing. No sign-off. No emojis. No em dashes.

assertions.name: under_400_chars
assertions.description: Message body is under 400 characters

assertions.name: exit_signal_present
assertions.description: Contains an exit signal: 'will stop reaching out', 'last one from me', 'won't keep going', or equivalent

assertions.name: no_call_ask_in_touch4
assertions.description: Does not ask for a call, DM chat, walkthrough, or meeting — Touch 4 is a breakup

assertions.name: soft_door_open
assertions.description: Leaves a door open for future reconnection without pressure, framed around his specific context

assertions.name: no_guilt_framing
assertions.description: Does not guilt-trip the lack of reply or imply effort was wasted

assertions.name: no_emojis
assertions.description: Contains no emoji characters

assertions.name: no_em_dashes
assertions.description: Contains no em dashes

assertions.name: no_sign_off
assertions.description: Does not end with '- Aleem' or a formal sign-off

assertions.name: no_following_up_phrases
assertions.description: Does not contain 'just following up', 'circling back', 'bumping this'

evals.name: scenario-b-casual-reply-label-phase
evals.prompt:
A prospect replied to my Touch 1. What do I send back?

Conversation:
[Me, Touch 1]: Hey Priya - it looks like you're scaling Lumen to the point where everything can't still run through you. What's the most painful part of that transition right now?

[Priya, same day]: lol honestly the client onboarding is killing us. every new client is like starting from scratch - different questions, different setup, nothing templated

Profile: Priya Sharma, @primasharma.lumen, Founder/CEO, Lumen (22-person B2B SaaS, HR tech). 2,900 followers.
evals.expected_output: A Scenario B output with the reply + meta-line (Phase / Tactic). This is the Label phase — she disclosed real pain casually, Aleem should mirror it back in slightly sharper language. At least one Voss label. Match her casual register (she said 'lol honestly' — don't respond stiffly). Should NOT drop a case study yet. Should NOT ask for a call. Should NOT pitch NexusPoint. Reply under 400 chars. Meta-line phase = Label or Deepen.

assertions.name: under_400_chars
assertions.description: Message body (excluding meta-line) is under 400 characters

assertions.name: contains_voss_label
assertions.description: Contains at least one Voss label: 'It sounds like', 'It looks like', 'It seems like', or 'It feels like'

assertions.name: matches_casual_register
assertions.description: Matches Priya's casual tone — does not respond with stiff professional language when she's texting informally

assertions.name: includes_meta_line
assertions.description: Output includes Phase: and Tactic: fields after a separator (---)

assertions.name: phase_is_label_or_deepen
assertions.description: Meta-line declares phase as Label or Deepen — not Proof, Warm Ask, or Call

assertions.name: no_case_study_yet
assertions.description: Does not drop a quantified NexusPoint result — she hasn't asked for proof yet

assertions.name: no_call_ask
assertions.description: Does not ask for a call, meeting, or DM walkthrough

assertions.name: no_nexuspoint_pitch
assertions.description: Does not pitch NexusPoint services or say 'I can build this for you'

assertions.name: label_sharpens_disclosure
assertions.description: The label reframes or sharpens what Priya said — not a verbatim repeat of her exact words

assertions.name: no_emojis
assertions.description: Contains no emoji characters

assertions.name: no_em_dashes
assertions.description: Contains no em dashes

assertions.name: no_sign_off
assertions.description: Does not end with '- Aleem' or a formal sign-off

evals.name: scenario-b-peer-consultant
evals.prompt:
This prospect looks like they're in the same space as me. What do I reply?

Conversation:
[Me, Touch 1]: Hey Jordan - it looks like you're doing interesting work at the intersection of AI and agency ops. What's the part of the build work you enjoy most?

[Jordan, same day]: Hey! Yeah I'm deep in that space. Mostly help agencies set up their AI workflows and automations. Looks like you're in similar territory - what are you building lately?

Profile: Jordan Blake, @jordanblake.ai, solo AI automation consultant, 5,200 followers. Bio: AI workflow design | helping agencies automate client delivery | Austin.
evals.expected_output: A Scenario B output with peer-track framing. Jordan does what Aleem does — he's not a client prospect. Aleem should NOT pitch services. Should treat Jordan as a peer and ask a calibrated question that surfaces build-side structure or partnership fit ('do you handle all the builds yourself or bring in specialists?'). Reply under 400 chars. Contains a Voss label. Meta-line phase = Open (peer track). Meta-line tactic flags the peer pivot explicitly.

assertions.name: under_400_chars
assertions.description: Message body (excluding meta-line) is under 400 characters

assertions.name: contains_voss_label
assertions.description: Contains at least one Voss label

assertions.name: no_client_pitch
assertions.description: Does not pitch NexusPoint services as if Jordan is a prospective client

assertions.name: no_client_call_ask
assertions.description: Does not ask Jordan to 'hop on a call' as a buyer

assertions.name: peer_or_partnership_framing
assertions.description: Frames the conversation as peer-to-peer or partnership territory — asks about build-side setup, overflow work, or collaboration model

assertions.name: calibrated_question_present
assertions.description: Contains a calibrated question (how/what) that surfaces Jordan's build-side setup or potential collaboration fit

assertions.name: includes_meta_line
assertions.description: Output includes Phase: and Tactic: fields after a separator

assertions.name: meta_flags_peer_track
assertions.description: Meta-line (Phase or Tactic) explicitly flags this is a peer/referral track, not a direct-client track

assertions.name: no_emojis
assertions.description: Contains no emoji characters

assertions.name: no_em_dashes
assertions.description: Contains no em dashes

assertions.name: no_sign_off
assertions.description: Does not end with '- Aleem' or a formal sign-off
